summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on February 11, 2025 at 6:00 PM. The board approved payment of bills, authorized a cleanup day at the old Green School property, adopted a policy to charge for video public records per Ohio House Bill 315, expanded acceptance of credit card payments via Resolution R-002-2025, and approved out-of-town training for Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan. The board also discussed picnic table replacement/refurbishment but deferred action pending further inspection, and adjourned into executive session regarding employee appointment, employment, or compensation.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (January 19, 2025 through February 11, 2025) – passed
Motion to approve cleanup day at the old Green School property – passed
Motion to charge for video public records as authorized by Ohio House Bill 315 at staff actual cost of $46.90 per hour – passed
Motion to approve Resolution R-002-2025 expanding acceptance of payments by financial transaction devices – passed
Picnic tables discussion – deferred pending further inspection by the service department
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan for supervisor training in Columbus, Ohio ($2,150 registration) – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:22 PM per ORC 121.22 – passed
Motion to adjourn the meeting at 7:46 PM – passed
action_items: Director Meadows/Chad Johnson to inspect the condition of picnic table frames (especially rust on bottoms) and report back at a future meeting
Service department to deliver wood chips to old Green School property for cleanup day
Chief Rose to implement video public records charging policy at $46.90/hour staff actual cost
Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan to attend three-week supervisor training and education program in Columbus, Ohio in 2025
key_amounts: $46.90 per hour – staff actual cost rate for charging video public records requests
$2,150 – registration cost for Detective Sergeant McMillan's supervisor training program
notable_topics: Staggy Road speed limit signs and speed study (previously passed resolution; signs now posted at 40 mph)
Cleanup day at old Green School property with owner permission and insurance coverage
Video public records charging policy under Ohio House Bill 315
Resolution R-002-2025 expanding credit card payment acceptance
Picnic table replacement/refurbishment discussion and deferral
Out-of-town travel approval for detective sergeant supervisor training
Executive session regarding appointment, employment, or compensation of a public employee
Community positivity and support including Fight for Megan's blue ribbons on police cars
Kevin Maloff nominated for Chamber Salute to Leaders
attendees: Kevin Maloff (mentioned, Chamber Salute to Leaders nominee)
notes: The transcript contains significant portions of garbled or corrupted text, likely due to automated captioning errors. The executive session lasted from approximately 6:22 PM to 7:46 PM. The board discussed a high flow machine for the service department that is on a 45-day order and may debut for cleanup day. Individual departments may purchase their own picnic tables independently of the board decision.
title: BOT 2-11-25
